strengths: 20 years of engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — clearly exceeds the 15-year minimum
Recent hands-on AI-agent work at re:cinq including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, showing engagement with current AI tooling
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, 2016–present), with a public talk at FOSS4G 2019
concerns: AI expertise is narrow and very recent (April 2025 only); no demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms built across the career prior to current role
No Master's or PhD in AI-related field; education is two Bachelor's degrees (CS and Fine Arts/Photography), leaving a significant gap against preferred qualifications

Career trajectory is consistently individual-contributor execution at senior/staff level — no evidence of advising strategic customers, setting org-wide technical direction, or operating at a director-level strategic scope
response.justification: The role demands recognized AI expertise with a portfolio of demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms, director-level strategic influence, and the ability to advise strategic customers — none of which the résumé evidences. The only AI work cited (MCP generators, agentic workflows) began in April 2025 and is described without outcomes, scale, or external recognition. Prior to 2025, the 19-year career is entirely in general software engineering (web, mobile, infrastructure, AdTech), with no ML, AI research, or AI product work. The educational profile carries no graduate AI credentials. The GISCollective founder role and conference talk show entrepreneurial instinct and knowledge-sharing willingness, which are genuine positives, but they do not close the gap on the core AI strategy and customer-advisory competencies this position requires. Score is derived from meeting the experience-duration bar and demonstrating early AI-adjacent work, offset by critical absences in AI depth, strategic scope, and educational credentials.
